results.label: Standard — neuroscience guest on productivity podcast
results.output_summary: Brief with all five sections. Guest Overview: 3 sentences on Dr. Webb and the attention myth argument. Key Work Summary explains the core claim — environment changed, not attention spans — and the evidence structure. Most Interesting Angle: the tension between his data and the 'goldfish attention span' claim that the entire productivity content industry relies on. Ground Already Covered: 4 points from written interviews. Suggested Questions: 6 open-ended questions including 'Where is the goldfish statistic actually from, and why won't it die?' Next Step: lead with question 3, run interview-question-builder to expand.

A1.note: All five sections present and well-developed.

A2.note: Angle identifies the specific tension: his work undermines the productivity content industry's foundational claim.

A3.note: 6 open-ended questions. Next Step recommends opening question and interview-question-builder.

results.label: Minimal — only required fields
results.output_summary: Brief adapted for a practitioner rather than an academic. Guest Overview frames Priya Sharma's 20-year restaurant experience. Key Work Summary focuses on the failure-and-recovery narrative arc. Most Interesting Angle: she has closed restaurants, which makes her more credible than the success-only guests. Ground Already Covered: noted as limited (no prior appearances specified). Suggested Questions: 5 questions focused on failure patterns.

A1.note: All five sections present. Ground Already Covered honestly notes limited data.

A2.note: Angle: her failure experience is her most valuable differentiator as a guest.

results.label: European context — EU energy policy expert
results.output_summary: Brief with EU policy context throughout. Guest Overview frames Dr. Braun's think tank role and energy transition focus. Key Work Summary explains the '2035 Gap' paper argument. Most Interesting Angle: the tension between technically achievable targets and politically impossible pathways. Topics-to-avoid respected (no mention of previous government advisory role). Suggested Questions: 7 questions including 'Which EU member state is most likely to break ranks on the 2035 targets, and what happens then?' Next Step: lead with question 1, run interview-question-builder.

A1.note: All five sections with European policy depth.

A2.note: Angle: the gap between technical feasibility and political will, which is the paper's core argument.

A3.note: 7 open-ended questions. Topics-to-avoid respected. Next Step present.
